name: Command A+
archDisplay: Sparse Mixture-of-Experts (218B total / 25B active), hybrid attention, native citation grounding, runs on 1x B200 or 2x H100 (W4A4)
note: Successor to the CC-BY-NC-licensed Command A (Mar 2025), now released fully open-weight under Apache 2.0. First Command model with native image input, expanded from 23 to 48 languages, and large agentic/enterprise gains (tau2-bench-telecom 37%->85%, Terminal-Bench-Hard agentic coding 3%->25%).

results.notes: up from 37% for Command A Reasoning

AIME 2025, official 30-question set, each question repeated 10 times, pass@1 over the 300-sample dataset (per the blog's own footnote methodology). Cohere reports an improvement from 57% (Command A Reasoning) to 90% (Command A+). The blog embeds the actual score only in a chart image, but the figure is corroborated verbatim by two independent secondary write-ups (codersera.com launch guide and a VentureBeat-sourced search summary), both citing the identical 57%->90% jump, which is strong convergent evidence for the primary vendor claim.
Artificial Analysis's independent GPQA Diamond re-evaluation of Command A+ (labeled 'AA-GPQA Diamond' on the aggregator). Not published by Cohere itself.
Artificial Analysis's independent Humanity's Last Exam score for Command A+ ('AA-HLE'). Not published by Cohere itself.
Artificial Analysis's independent IFBench score for Command A+ ('AA-IFBench'). Not published by Cohere itself.
Vals AI independent evaluation, MMLU Pro accuracy 78.15%. Not previously tracked (existing entries cover mmmu/mmmu-pro/mathvista/charxiv/aime/gpqa/hle/ifbench/tau2-bench-telecom, but not mmlu-pro).
Vals AI independent evaluation, LiveCodeBench accuracy 79.38%. Genuinely new coding-benchmark data point; not currently tracked for Command A+.

Vals AI independent evaluation, listed as 'Terminal-Bench 2.1' (verified refresh of Terminal-Bench 2.0 with patched Dockerfiles/instruction fixes) accuracy 17.60%. Mapped to taxonomy's terminal-bench-2 slug since there is no distinct 2.1 slug. Note this is separate from 'Terminal-Bench Hard' (25%, cited in Cohere's own launch blog) which is a genuinely different, harder benchmark variant not in the taxonomy list -- deliberately not force-mapped to avoid variant conflation.
SealedBench sealed capability index — composite of capability, token efficiency, speed and cost; rank 27/28 on the 2026-07-22 run (sealedbench.com). Listed as "Command A+" (Cohere).

verificationNotes:
Confirmed via Cohere's own blog post (cohere.com/blog/command-a-plus), independent coverage on MarkTechPost (2026-05-21), and the CohereLabs Hugging Face org (bf16/fp8/w4a4 repos for 'command-a-plus-05-2026'), which confirms Apache 2.0 license, 218B/25B MoE, 128K input / 64K output context, and 48 languages. This is a genuine gap: the existing corpus's Cohere folder stops at 'Command A' (2025-03-13, CC-BY-NC); Command A+ is a distinct, newer, more permissively licensed release.

Round 22 gap-chase for 'swe-bench' (SWE-bench Verified) on Command A+ (218B, Cohere, released 2026-05-20): thoroughly searched and found NO citable score anywhere; treating as a confirmed absence rather than an unfound value. Checked 5 of the 6 required methods: (1) Official launch materials -- re-read cohere.com/blog/command-a-plus in full via rendered browser fetch; its footnoted benchmark table covers tau2-Bench Telecom, Terminal-Bench Hard, AIME 2025, IFBench, MMMU/MMMU-Pro, MathVista, CharXiv, MT-AIME, WMT24++, and the AA Intelligence Index -- SWE-bench is never mentioned. (2) Independent trackers: Artificial Analysis's own Command A+ model page (artificialanalysis.ai, rendered via browser) confirms their Intelligence Index v4.1 coding coverage uses Terminal-Bench v2.1 + SciCode, NOT SWE-bench -- so AA has no SWE-bench number for this model. Vals.ai's Command A+ model page (vals.ai/models/cohere_command-a-plus-05-2026, rendered via browser) lists its full 16-benchmark suite (Vals Index, Vals Multimodal Index, CorpFin v2, Finance Agent v2, MedCode, MedScribe, MortgageTax, SAGE, TaxEval v2, Vibe Code Bench v1.1, GPQA Diamond, LiveCodeBench, LegalBench, MMLU Pro, MMMU, Terminal-Bench 2.1) -- no SWE-bench Verified entry. Cross-checked from the other direction on vals.ai's own SWE-bench Verified leaderboard (vals.ai/benchmarks/swebench) filtered to the COHERE organization: only 'Command A' (the older 111B model, already tracked in this corpus's sibling file at 7.8%) appears in the roster of 71 evaluated systems -- 'Command A+' is absent. BenchLM.ai's Command A+ profile (benchlm.ai/models/command-a-plus) lists 15 benchmark scores across agentic/coding/reasoning/knowledge/multimodal categories with an explicit note that SWE-bench and SWE-bench Verified are not among them. llm-stats.com's SWE-Bench Verified leaderboard (104 self-reported models, 0 verified) was paged through its top 50 entries (scores 0.725-0.950) with no Cohere model appearing at all. swebench.com's own official leaderboard (swebench.com/verified.html) could not be data-extracted via the fetch tools available (client-rendered table), but no secondary source anywhere corroborates a Command A+ entry there either. (3) Academic/GitHub: targeted arXiv/GitHub searches for 'command-a-plus' + swebench/SWE-bench turned up nothing beyond the HF repo and the SWE-bench project itself. (4) Sibling-model comparison tables: Cohere's very next coding-focused release, North Mini Code (2026-06-09, cohere.com/blog/north-mini-code), does report its own SWE-Bench Verified (67.6) and SWE-Bench Pro (40.2) scores -- confirmed this was the most promising sibling-citation lead -- but its blog post and HF model card (huggingface.co/CohereLabs/North-Mini-Code-1.0) do not cite Command A or Command A+ anywhere, let alone with a SWE-bench number; the comparison set there is other small open coding models (Devstral Small 2, Nemotron 3 Super, Mistral Small 4), not Command A+. Conclusion: SWE-bench Verified has genuinely not been run/reported for Command A+ by Cohere or by any of the independent trackers that do cover it for contemporaries -- this is a real coverage gap in the ecosystem, not a research shortfall, so proposing omitted_no_evidence rather than inventing or estimating a number.
